skill.name: notebooklm-manager
skill.description: Manage NotebookLM notebooks from Claude Code through a Chrome-based connector. The skill registers notebook URLs, queries notebooks, performs follow-up coverage checks, and stores notebook metadata in a local skill data folder.
skill.summary: Manage and query NotebookLM notebooks through Claude Code and Chrome.
skill.author: LeeJuOh

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review shows these are Markdown inline code blocks, JSON examples, and documentation text rather than executable code. The real risk is medium because this community skill delegates browser automation to a NotebookLM Chrome subagent, accesses user NotebookLM content, and writes notebook registry data under the skill data directory.

medium_findings.title: Delegated Browser Automation Can Access NotebookLM Content
medium_findings.description: The skill invokes a NotebookLM Chrome subagent with a user-provided notebook URL and question. This is expected behavior, but it can expose sensitive notebook content through browser automation and should require user trust in the connector.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Task delegation is explicit and passes the notebook URL, question, mode, and clearHistory setting. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it creates a real privacy and browser-session access risk.

medium_findings.title: Local Notebook Registry Writes User Notebook Metadata
medium_findings.description: The skill creates and updates local data files for notebook URLs, IDs, topics, archive state, and metadata. This is useful functionality, but it stores potentially sensitive research references on disk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The storage paths and fields are documented clearly, including notebook URLs and topics. The writes appear constrained to the skill data directory, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded NotebookLM URL Patterns
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Google NotebookLM examples and expected NotebookLM notebook links. They are not evidence of exfiltration to an unknown third party.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every hardcoded URL is a NotebookLM domain or example field aligned with the skill purpose. No evidence found of covert network destinations or credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged inline code, fenced examples, table command names, and template placeholders as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are documentation examples, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown documentation, and the flagged backticks delimit code formatting or examples. No executable Ruby, shell scripts, or command invocation code is present in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings appear to match Markdown file syntax, NotebookLM wording, or JSON snippets. No hash function, cipher call, or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain front matter, option labels, command documentation, and example JSON. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are File-State Instructions
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged file existence and initialization instructions as reconnaissance. The content only describes how to create missing local registry files for the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions concern the skill data folder and specific JSON files. No evidence found of host profiling, environment harvesting, or broad filesystem discovery.

dangerous_patterns.title: Task Delegation to Chrome Subagent
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill delegates NotebookLM interaction to a named Chrome MCP query subagent. This pattern is powerful and should be monitored because it relies on browser session state and external web content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subagent invocation is explicit and central to the workflow. It is not malicious by itself, but it is the primary sensitive behavior in this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Local Notebook Metadata
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill stores NotebookLM URLs, IDs, topics, and archive information under the skill root data directory. Users should understand this persistence before adding private notebooks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The schema examples and storage instructions confirm persistent metadata. The scope appears limited and documented, which keeps the risk at medium.

content.user_title: Manage NotebookLM Research from Claude Code
content.value_statement: NotebookLM research often spans many notebooks, URLs, and follow-up questions. This skill helps Claude Code register notebooks, query them through Chrome, and organize cited answers.
actual_capabilities: Lists active and archived NotebookLM notebook entries from a local registry.
Adds NotebookLM notebook URLs and can discover notebook metadata through a Chrome subagent.
Queries a selected notebook and requests cited answers from NotebookLM.
Runs follow-up coverage checks when the first response misses requested topics.
Supports showing, searching, enabling, disabling, and removing notebook records.
Stores notebook metadata in library, archive, and per-notebook JSON files.
limitations: Requires Claude Code with Chrome integration and a connected browser session.
Requires the user to be logged in to Google for NotebookLM access.
Does not perform general web search or local document reading outside NotebookLM workflows.
Quality depends on NotebookLM responses and the availability of notebook source citations.

use_cases.title: Researcher Notebook Q&A
use_cases.description: Ask a registered NotebookLM notebook focused questions and receive synthesized answers with citations.

use_cases.title: Team Knowledge Registry
use_cases.description: Maintain a searchable list of NotebookLM notebooks for projects, documentation sets, and shared research collections.

use_cases.title: Source Coverage Review
use_cases.description: Check whether NotebookLM answers cover each requested topic, then trigger follow-up queries for missing areas.

prompt_templates.title: List Notebooks
prompt_templates.prompt: List my NotebookLM notebooks and show the active notebook IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Add Notebook
prompt_templates.prompt: Add this NotebookLM URL and discover its title, topics, and source documents: https://notebooklm.google.com/notebook/...

prompt_templates.title: Ask Notebook
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask my claude-docs notebook what it says about authentication setup. Include citations when available.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Query my product-research notebook about pricing, onboarding risks, and customer objections. Run follow-ups for any missing topics before summarizing.

output_examples.output: Shows active notebook IDs, notebook names, topics, and whether archived notebooks are included.

output_examples.input: Ask my policy notebook about retention requirements.
output_examples.output: Returns the notebook title, a concise answer, source citations when NotebookLM provides them, and suggested follow-up questions.
Creates a registry entry with a generated ID, discovered title, topics, and a short confirmation summary.

best_practices: Register notebooks with clear IDs that match their topic or project.
Review stored NotebookLM URLs before sharing a workspace or skill data folder.
Ask focused questions and request follow-up coverage for multi-topic research.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for general web searches unrelated to NotebookLM.
Do not add private notebook URLs in shared environments without checking local storage access.
Do not expect the skill to bypass Google login, NotebookLM permissions, or browser connection requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill require Chrome?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects Claude Code Chrome integration and a connected Chrome or Edge browser session.

faq.question: Can it read private NotebookLM notebooks?
faq.answer: It can interact with notebooks available in the connected browser session. Users should treat those responses as sensitive.

faq.question: Where does it store notebook information?
faq.answer: It stores registry and metadata files under the skill data directory described in the skill instructions.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found of shell command execution in the scanned files. The command alerts were Markdown formatting false positives.

faq.question: Can it clear NotebookLM chat history?
faq.answer: It asks the user before the first query to a notebook, then passes the selected setting to the Chrome subagent.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses browser automation and stores notebook metadata locally.
